svg.text: Cross-cloud topology • DR / SPOF / Load-test scenarios • GKE workload ownership • NFL / Super Bowl readiness
Sources: GCP Foundational Setup (4127949709) · CCI Detailed (4346807036) · Engine V1 Infra (5415108609) · SB 2026 Retro (4965433382)

g.text: Cloud DNS · GLB · Cloud Armor · GAR · WIF · IAM · Spanner
VPC · Subnets · Cloud SQL · Pub/Sub · Regional LB · GKE control plane
GCE / GKE node pools · PD disks · MIG instance
8 regions · NFL peak ~128k RPS ingress · Pingify / STUN / WebSocket / HTTPS
CDN · WAF · public DNS · DDoS shield
v1 customer envs → AWS · v2 / SB / NFL traffic → GCP · fallbacks both ways
Account per customer env · TGW-peered · Cloudflare-fronted
Static routes for MySQL VIPs · BGP to GCP cloud routers (via CCI & VPN)
Built w/ Packer AMIs + Ansible · Blue-green via EIP swap
App tier (PHP / Laravel / Node on EC2)
Multi-arch · Helm + ArgoCD · KEDA HPA · Istio mesh
L4 entry · mTLS · cert rotation via Secrets Mgr
↔ GCP via WIF (gcp-workload-identity TF module) for cross-cloud auth
↔ AWS-side WIF identity provider for GKE → SQS / DynamoDB
Mixed AWS-native + cross-stack (LGTM mirrors GCP side)
1 HAProxy VIP · 2 Consul quorum · 3 MySQL master VIP
4 EC2 AMI drift · 5 Single EKS cell loss · 6 TGW peer loss
7 Region loss (us-east-1) → fail to sa-east-1 / GCP
All numbered ⃝ markers map to runbook entries
• Migrate v1 VM stack to GCP first (faster path) — reuse GCP-DR IaC modules
• Finish NLB+Istio rollout across all cells; remove HAProxy where green
• EFS for MaxMind / GeoStream — confirm IOPS profile under load test
• Cross-cloud WIF coverage for every service touching GCP managed data
geocomply.com → gc-v2 → production · pre-prod · staging · dev · platform · sandbox
VPC + subnets per region · /18 pod CIDR per cluster
Bottleneck: 65 nodes / cluster at /18 → tracked for SB scaling
Shared by all service projects (workloads-prod, bootstrap-prod, etc.)
Pre-scale (SB): pools at max · ≥16 Spanner nodes
Drains via Cloud DNS weighted routing
Managed data plane (GCP v2 priority: managed services)
STUN/Coturn (4→17 VMs pre-scale) · Pingify · ICMP · WebSocket · HTTPS · 0% L7 errors at SB peak
All envs (prod, pre-prod, staging, dev) reuse same modules · IaC = github.com/GeoComply/terraform-modules
8 us-east4 cluster loss → drain via Cloud DNS to us-central1 · validate ArgoCD reconcile time
9 Spanner CPU saturation (open: hit 65% at SB, target ≤45%) — pre-scale nodes / cap traffic
10 Pod-IP /18 ceiling (65 nodes/cluster) — add more clusters or expand CIDR before NFL 2026-27
+ DIS metric leak (open from SB retro) — fix before next load test
+ Cloud Armor rule push (test in pre-prod first; staged WAF rollout)
Map of team → service → today vs target placement · pool
Owners (lead names) intentionally blank — each team to fill in their primary + backup contact during review.
Pool naming follows GCP Foundational Setup (app + system + bootstrap-specific pools).
For Engine v1 → GCP migration sequencing see NFL 2026-2027 Preparation doc (5287804979).
Use the numbered ⃝ markers as failure-injection
MySQL, EKS cell, CCI, GKE cluster, Spanner.
Drop each ⃝ point in isolation; verify auto-recovery
vs. manual runbook. Worst offenders: MySQL master
VIP (3), Consul quorum (2), single EKS cell (5),
SB peak ref: ~128k RPS ingress · ~133k aggregate
GKE pools at max, Cloud Armor rules pre-staged.
Single canvas for NFL / SB exec readouts.
Each team owns its row in the ownership table
and the matching bordered region in the diagram.

svg.text: Template — engineers iterate per team. Each ⃝ marker maps to a runbook step. Diagram canvas: 1500×1600. Icons are inline <symbol> defs — edit colors / shapes in any vector tool.
